A trust shall not constitute a qualified trust under this section unless the plan of which such trust is a part provides that in the case of any merger or consolidation with, or transfer of assets or liabilities to, any other plan after , each participant in the plan would (if the plan then terminated) receive a benefit immediately after the merger, consolidation, or transfer which is equal to or greater than the benefit he would have been entitled to receive immediately before the merger, consolidation, or transfer (if the plan had then terminated). The preceding sentence does not apply to any multiemployer plan with respect to any transaction to the extent that participants either before or after the transaction are covered under a multiemployer plan to which title IV of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 applies.September 2, 1974

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: A trust shall not constitute a qualified trust under this section unless the plan of which such trust is a part provides that benefits provided under the plan may not be assigned or alienated. For purposes of the preceding sentence, there shall not be taken into account any voluntary and revocable assignment of not to exceed 10 percent of any benefit payment made by any participant who is receiving benefits under the plan unless the assignment or alienation is made for purposes of defraying plan administration costs. For purposes of this paragraph a loan made to a participant or beneficiary shall not be treated as an assignment or alienation if such loan is secured by the participant’s accrued nonforfeitable benefit and is exempt from the tax imposed by section 4975 (relating to tax on prohibited transactions) by reason of section 4975(d)(1). This paragraph shall take effect on and shall not apply to assignments which were irrevocable on .January 1, 1976September 2, 1974

children.heading: .—Special rules for domestic relations orders
children.label: B .—Special rules for domestic relations orders
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall apply to the creation, assignment, or recognition of a right to any benefit payable with respect to a participant pursuant to a domestic relations order, except that subparagraph (A) shall not apply if the order is determined to be a qualified domestic relations order.

children.heading: .—Special rule for certain judgments and settlements
children.label: C .—Special rule for certain judgments and settlements
I
children.text: under a judgment of conviction for a crime involving such plan,

children.label: II
children.text: under a civil judgment (including a consent order or decree) entered by a court in an action brought in connection with a violation (or alleged violation) of part 4 of subtitle B of title I of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974, or

children.label: III
children.text: pursuant to a settlement agreement between the Secretary of Labor and the participant, or a settlement agreement between the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation and the participant, in connection with a violation (or alleged violation) of part 4 of such subtitle by a fiduciary or any other person,

children.label: ii
children.text: the judgment, order, decree, or settlement agreement expressly provides for the offset of all or part of the amount ordered or required to be paid to the plan against the participant’s benefits provided under the plan, and

children.label: I
children.text: either such spouse has consented in writing to such offset and such consent is witnessed by a notary public or representative of the plan (or it is established to the satisfaction of a plan representative that such consent may not be obtained by reason of circumstances described in section 417(a)(2)(B)), or an election to waive the right of the spouse to either a qualified joint and survivor annuity or a qualified preretirement survivor annuity is in effect in accordance with the requirements of section 417(a),

children.label: II
children.text: such spouse is ordered or required in such judgment, order, decree, or settlement to pay an amount to the plan in connection with a violation of part 4 of such subtitle, or

children.label: III
children.text: in such judgment, order, decree, or settlement, such spouse retains the right to receive the survivor annuity under a qualified joint and survivor annuity provided pursuant to section 401(a)(11)(A)(i) and under a qualified preretirement survivor annuity provided pursuant to section 401(a)(11)(A)(ii), determined in accordance with subparagraph (D).

children.label: I
children.text: the participant terminated employment on the date of the offset,

children.label: II
children.text: there was no offset,

children.label: III
children.text: the plan permitted commencement of benefits only on or after normal retirement age,

children.label: IV
children.text: the plan provided only the minimum-required qualified joint and survivor annuity, and

children.label: V
children.text: the amount of the qualified preretirement survivor annuity under the plan is equal to the amount of the survivor annuity payable under the minimum-required qualified joint and survivor annuity.

children.heading: .—Definition
children.label: ii .—Definition
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “minimum-required qualified joint and survivor annuity” means the qualified joint and survivor annuity which is the actuarial equivalent of the participant’s accrued benefit (within the meaning of section 411(a)(7)) and under which the survivor annuity is 50 percent of the amount of the annuity which is payable during the joint lives of the participant and the spouse.

children.label: A
children.text: the date on which the participant attains the earlier of age 65 or the normal retirement age specified under the plan,

children.label: B
children.text: occurs the 10th anniversary of the year in which the participant commenced participation in the plan, or

children.label: C
children.text: the participant terminates his service with the employer.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a participant or beneficiary who is receiving benefits under such plan, or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a participant who is separated from the service and who has nonforfeitable rights to benefits,
A trust shall not constitute a qualified trust under this section if the plan of which such trust is a part provides for benefits or contributions which exceed the limitations of section 415.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: A trust shall not constitute a qualified trust under this section unless, under the plan of which such trust is a part, the annual compensation of each employee taken into account under the plan for any year does not exceed $200,000.

children.heading: .—Cost-of-living adjustment
children.label: B .—Cost-of-living adjustment
children.text: The Secretary shall adjust annually the $200,000 amount in subparagraph (A) for increases in the cost-of-living at the same time and in the same manner as adjustments under section 415(d); except that the base period shall be the calendar quarter beginning , and any increase which is not a multiple of $5,000 shall be rounded to the next lowest multiple of $5,000.July 1, 2001
Repealed. , , .]Pub. L. 97–248, title II, § 237(b)96 Stat. 511Sept. 3, 1982

A trust shall not constitute a qualified trust under this section if under the plan of which such trust is a part any part of a participant’s accrued benefit derived from employer contributions (whether or not otherwise nonforfeitable), is forfeitable solely because of withdrawal by such participant of any amount attributable to the benefit derived from contributions made by such participant. The preceding sentence shall not apply to the accrued benefit of any participant unless, at the time of such withdrawal, such participant has a nonforfeitable right to at least 50 percent of such accrued benefit (as determined under section 411). The first sentence of this paragraph shall not apply to the extent that an accrued benefit is permitted to be forfeited in accordance with section 411(a)(3)(D)(iii) (relating to proportional forfeitures of benefits accrued before , in the event of withdrawal of certain mandatory contributions).September 2, 1974

A trust forming part of a pension plan shall not be treated as failing to constitute a qualified trust under this section merely because the pension plan of which such trust is a part makes 1 or more distributions within 1 taxable year to a distributee on account of a termination of the plan of which the trust is a part, or in the case of a profit-sharing or stock bonus plan, a complete discontinuance of contributions under such plan. This paragraph shall not apply to a defined benefit plan unless the employer maintaining such plan files a notice with the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation (at the time and in the manner prescribed by the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation) notifying the Corporation of such payment or distribution and the Corporation has approved such payment or distribution or, within 90 days after the date on which such notice was filed, has failed to disapprove such payment or distribution. For purposes of this paragraph, rules similar to the rules of section 402(a)(6)(B) (as in effect before its repeal by section 521 of the Unemployment Compensation Amendments of 1992) shall apply.

children.label: A
children.text: is established by an employer whose stock is not readily tradable on an established market, and

children.label: B
children.text: after acquiring securities of the employer, more than 10 percent of the total assets of the plan are securities of the employer,

A stock bonus plan shall not be treated as meeting the requirements of this section unless such plan meets the requirements of subsections (h) and () of section 409, except that in applying section 409(h) for purposes of this paragraph, the term “employer securities” shall include any securities of the employer held by the plan.o

Any group trust which otherwise meets the requirements of this section shall not be treated as not meeting such requirements on account of the participation or inclusion in such trust of the moneys of any plan or governmental unit described in section 818(a)(6).

children.heading: .—Requirement that actuarial assumptions be specified
children.label: 25 .—Requirement that actuarial assumptions be specified
children.text: A defined benefit plan shall not be treated as providing definitely determinable benefits unless, whenever the amount of any benefit is to be determined on the basis of actuarial assumptions, such assumptions are specified in the plan in a way which precludes employer discretion.

children.label: i
children.text: 50 employees of the employer, or

children.label: I
children.text: 40 percent of all employees of the employer, or

children.label: II
children.text: 2 employees (or if there is only 1 employee, such employee).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: A plan may exclude from consideration under this paragraph employees described in paragraphs (3) and (4)(A) of section 410(b).

children.heading: .—Separate application for certain excludable employees
children.label: ii .—Separate application for certain excludable employees
I
children.text: the benefits for such employees are provided under the same plan as benefits for other employees,

children.label: II
children.text: the benefits provided to such employees are not greater than comparable benefits provided to other employees under the plan, and

children.label: III
children.text: no highly compensated employee (within the meaning of section 414(q)) is included in the group of such employees for more than 1 year.

children.heading: .—Special rule for collective bargaining units
children.label: C .—Special rule for collective bargaining units
children.text: Except to the extent provided in regulations, a plan covering only employees described in section 410(b)(3)(A) may exclude from consideration any employees who are not included in the unit or units in which the covered employees are included.

children.heading: .—Paragraph not to apply to multiemployer plans
children.label: D .—Paragraph not to apply to multiemployer plans
children.text: Except to the extent provided in regulations, this paragraph shall not apply to employees in a multiemployer plan (within the meaning of section 414(f)) who are covered by collective bargaining agreements.

children.heading: .—Special rule for certain dispositions or acquisitions
children.label: E .—Special rule for certain dispositions or acquisitions
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 410(b)(6)(C) shall apply for purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: .—Separate lines of business
children.label: F .—Separate lines of business
children.text: At the election of the employer and with the consent of the Secretary, this paragraph may be applied separately with respect to each separate line of business of the employer. For purposes of this paragraph, the term “separate line of business” has the meaning given such term by section 414(r) (without regard to paragraph (2)(A) or (7) thereof).

children.heading: .—Exception for governmental plans
children.label: G .—Exception for governmental plans
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to a governmental plan (within the meaning of section 414(d)).

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: H .—Regulations
children.text: The Secretary may by regulation provide that any separate benefit structure, any separate trust, or any other separate arrangement is to be treated as a separate plan for purposes of applying this paragraph.

children.label: aa
children.text: to cease all benefit accruals, or

children.label: bb
children.text: to provide future benefit accruals only to a closed class of participants,

children.label: II
children.text: the plan satisfies subparagraph (A) (without regard to this subparagraph) as of the effective date of the amendment, and

children.label: III
children.text: the amendment was adopted before , or the plan is described in clause (ii).April 5, 2017

children.heading: .—Plans described
children.label: ii .—Plans described
children.text: A plan is described in this clause if the plan would be described in subsection ()(1)(C), as applied for purposes of subsection ()(1)(B)(iii)(IV) and by treating the effective date of the amendment as the date the class was closed for purposes of subsection ()(1)(C).ooo

children.heading: .—Special rules
children.label: iii .—Special rules
children.text: For purposes of clause (i)(II), in applying section 410(b)(6)(C), the amendments described in clause (i) shall not be treated as a significant change in coverage under section 410(b)(6)(C)(i)(II).

children.heading: .—Spun-off plans
children.label: iv .—Spun-off plans
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, if a portion of a plan described in clause (i) is spun off to another employer, the treatment under clause (i) of the spun-off plan shall continue with respect to the other employer.

children.heading: .—Contributions need not be based on profits
children.label: A .—Contributions need not be based on profits
children.text: The determination of whether the plan under which any contributions are made is a profit-sharing plan shall be made without regard to current or accumulated profits of the employer and without regard to whether the employer is a tax-exempt organization.

children.heading: .—Plan must designate type
children.label: B .—Plan must designate type
children.text: In the case of a plan which is intended to be a money purchase pension plan or a profit-sharing plan, a trust forming part of such plan shall not constitute a qualified trust under this subsection unless the plan designates such intent at such time and in such manner as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.heading: Additional requirements relating to employee stock ownership plans.—
children.label: 28 Additional requirements relating to employee stock ownership plans.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: In the case of a trust which is part of an employee stock ownership plan (within the meaning of section 4975(e)(7)) or a plan which meets the requirements of section 409(a), such trust shall not constitute a qualified trust under this section unless such plan meets the requirements of subparagraphs (B) and (C).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: A plan meets the requirements of this subparagraph if each qualified participant in the plan may elect within 90 days after the close of each plan year in the qualified election period to direct the plan as to the investment of at least 25 percent of the participant’s account in the plan (to the extent such portion exceeds the amount to which a prior election under this subparagraph applies). In the case of the election year in which the participant can make his last election, the preceding sentence shall be applied by substituting “50 percent” for “25 percent”.

children.label: I
children.text: the portion of the participant’s account covered by the election under clause (i) is distributed within 90 days after the period during which the election may be made, or

children.label: II
children.text: the plan offers at least 3 investment options (not inconsistent with regulations prescribed by the Secretary) to each participant making an election under clause (i) and within 90 days after the period during which the election may be made, the plan invests the portion of the participant’s account covered by the election in accordance with such election.

children.heading: .—Qualified participant
children.label: iii .—Qualified participant
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “qualified participant” means any employee who has completed at least 10 years of participation under the plan and has attained age 55.

children.label: I
children.text: the 1st plan year in which the individual first became a qualified participant, or

children.label: II
children.text: the 1st plan year beginning after .December 31, 1986

children.heading: .—Exception
children.label: v .—Exception
children.text: This subparagraph shall not apply to an applicable defined contribution plan (as defined in paragraph (35)(E)).

children.heading: .—Use of independent appraiser
children.label: C .—Use of independent appraiser
children.text: A plan meets the requirements of this subparagraph if all valuations of employer securities which are not readily tradable on an established securities market with respect to activities carried on by the plan are by an independent appraiser. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term “independent appraiser” means any appraiser meeting requirements similar to the requirements of the regulations prescribed under section 170(a)(1).

children.heading: .—Benefit limitations
children.label: 29 .—Benefit limitations
children.text: In the case of a defined benefit plan (other than a multiemployer plan or a CSEC plan) to which the requirements of section 412 apply, the trust of which the plan is a part shall not constitute a qualified trust under this subsection unless the plan meets the requirements of section 436.

children.heading: .—Limitations on elective deferrals
children.label: 30 .—Limitations on elective deferrals
children.text: In the case of a trust which is part of a plan under which elective deferrals (within the meaning of section 402(g)(3)) may be made with respect to any individual during a calendar year, such trust shall not constitute a qualified trust under this subsection unless the plan provides that the amount of such deferrals under such plan and all other plans, contracts, or arrangements of an employer maintaining such plan may not exceed the amount of the limitation in effect under section 402(g)(1)(A) for taxable years beginning in such calendar year.

children.heading: Direct transfer of eligible rollover distributions.—
children.label: 31 Direct transfer of eligible rollover distributions.—
i
children.text: elects to have such distribution paid directly to an eligible retirement plan, and

children.label: ii
children.text: specifies the eligible retirement plan to which such distribution is to be paid (in such form and at such time as the plan administrator may prescribe),

children.label: I
children.text: a distribution described in clause (ii) in excess of $1,000 is made, and

children.label: II
children.text: the distributee does not make an election under subparagraph (A) and does not elect to receive the distribution directly,

children.heading: .—Eligible plan
children.label: ii .—Eligible plan
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the term “eligible plan” means a plan which provides that any nonforfeitable accrued benefit for which the present value (as determined under section 411(a)(11)) does not exceed $7,000 shall be immediately distributed to the participant.

children.label: i
children.text: is a qualified trust which is part of a plan which is a defined contribution plan and agrees to separately account for amounts so transferred, including separately accounting for the portion of such distribution which is includible in gross income and the portion of such distribution which is not so includible, or

children.label: ii
children.text: is an eligible retirement plan described in clause (i) or (ii) of section 402(c)(8)(B).

children.heading: .—Eligible rollover distribution
children.label: D .—Eligible rollover distribution
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “eligible rollover distribution” has the meaning given such term by section 402(f)(2)(A).

children.heading: .—Eligible retirement plan
children.label: E .—Eligible retirement plan
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “eligible retirement plan” has the meaning given such term by section 402(c)(8)(B), except that a qualified trust shall be considered an eligible retirement plan only if it is a defined contribution plan, the terms of which permit the acceptance of rollover distributions.

children.heading: Treatment of failure to make certain payments if plan has liquidity shortfall.—
children.label: 32 Treatment of failure to make certain payments if plan has liquidity shortfall.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: A trust forming part of a pension plan to which section 430(j)(4) or 433(f)(5) applies shall not be treated as failing to constitute a qualified trust under this section merely because such plan ceases to make any payment described in subparagraph (B) during any period that such plan has a liquidity shortfall (as defined in section 430(j)(4) or 433(f)(5)).

children.label: i
children.text: any payment, in excess of the monthly amount paid under a single life annuity (plus any social security supplements described in the last sentence of section 411(a)(9)), to a participant or beneficiary whose annuity starting date (as defined in section 417(f)(2)) occurs during the period referred to in subparagraph (A),

children.label: ii
children.text: any payment for the purchase of an irrevocable commitment from an insurer to pay benefits, and

children.label: iii
children.text: any other payment specified by the Secretary by regulations.

children.heading: .—Period of shortfall
children.label: C .—Period of shortfall
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, a plan has a liquidity shortfall during the period that there is an underpayment of an installment under section 430(j)(3) or 433(f) by reason of section 430(j)(4)(A) or 433(f)(5), respectively.

children.heading: Prohibition on benefit increases while sponsor is in bankruptcy.—
children.label: 33 Prohibition on benefit increases while sponsor is in bankruptcy.—
i
children.text: any increase in benefits,

children.label: ii
children.text: any change in the accrual of benefits, or

children.label: iii
children.text: any change in the rate at which benefits become nonforfeitable under the plan,

children.label: i
children.text: the plan, were such amendment to take effect, would have a funding target attainment percentage (as defined in section 430(d)(2)) of 100 percent or more,

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary determines that such amendment is reasonable and provides for only de minimis increases in the liabilities of the plan with respect to employees of the debtor,

children.label: iii
children.text: such amendment only repeals an amendment described in section 412(d)(2), or

children.label: iv
children.text: such amendment is required as a condition of qualification under this part.

children.heading: .—Plans to which this paragraph applies
children.label: C .—Plans to which this paragraph applies
children.text: This paragraph shall apply only to plans (other than multiemployer plans or CSEC plans) covered under section 4021 of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974.

children.heading: .—Employer
children.label: D .—Employer
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “employer” means the employer referred to in section 412(b)(1), without regard to section 412(b)(2).

children.heading: .—Benefits of missing participants on plan termination
children.label: 34 .—Benefits of missing participants on plan termination
children.text: In the case of a plan covered by title IV of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974, a trust forming part of such plan shall not be treated as failing to constitute a qualified trust under this section merely because the pension plan of which such trust is a part, upon its termination, transfers benefits of missing participants to the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation in accordance with section 4050 of such Act.

children.heading: Diversification requirements for certain defined contribution plans.—
children.label: 35 Diversification requirements for certain defined contribution plans.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: A trust which is part of an applicable defined contribution plan shall not be treated as a qualified trust unless the plan meets the diversification requirements of subparagraphs (B), (C), and (D).

children.heading: .—Employee contributions and elective deferrals invested in employer securities
children.label: B .—Employee contributions and elective deferrals invested in employer securities
children.text: In the case of the portion of an applicable individual’s account attributable to employee contributions and elective deferrals which is invested in employer securities, a plan meets the requirements of this subparagraph if the applicable individual may elect to direct the plan to divest any such securities and to reinvest an equivalent amount in other investment options meeting the requirements of subparagraph (D).

children.heading: .—Employer contributions invested in employer securities
children.label: C .—Employer contributions invested in employer securities
i
children.text: is a participant who has completed at least 3 years of service, or

children.label: ii
children.text: is a beneficiary of a participant described in clause (i) or of a deceased participant,

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: The requirements of this subparagraph are met if the plan offers not less than 3 investment options, other than employer securities, to which an applicable individual may direct the proceeds from the divestment of employer securities pursuant to this paragraph, each of which is diversified and has materially different risk and return characteristics.

children.heading: Treatment of certain restrictions and conditions.—
children.label: ii Treatment of certain restrictions and conditions.—

children.heading: .—Time for making investment choices
children.label: I .—Time for making investment choices
children.text: A plan shall not be treated as failing to meet the requirements of this subparagraph merely because the plan limits the time for divestment and reinvestment to periodic, reasonable opportunities occurring no less frequently than quarterly.

children.heading: .—Certain restrictions and conditions not allowed
children.label: II .—Certain restrictions and conditions not allowed
children.text: Except as provided in regulations, a plan shall not meet the requirements of this subparagraph if the plan imposes restrictions or conditions with respect to the investment of employer securities which are not imposed on the investment of other assets of the plan. This subclause shall not apply to any restrictions or conditions imposed by reason of the application of securities laws.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: The term “applicable defined contribution plan” means any defined contribution plan which holds any publicly traded employer securities.

children.label: I
children.text: there are no contributions to such plan (or earnings thereunder) which are held within such plan and are subject to subsection (k) or (m), and

children.label: II
children.text: such plan is a separate plan for purposes of section 414() with respect to any other defined benefit plan or defined contribution plan maintained by the same employer or employers.l

children.heading: .—Exception for one participant plans
children.label: iii .—Exception for one participant plans
children.text: Such term does not include a one-participant retirement plan.

children.label: I
children.text: covered only one individual (or the individual and the individual’s spouse) and the individual (or the individual and the individual’s spouse) owned 100 percent of the plan sponsor (whether or not incorporated), or

children.label: II
children.text: covered only one or more partners (or partners and their spouses) in the plan sponsor.

children.heading: Certain plans treated as holding publicly traded employer securities.—
children.label: F Certain plans treated as holding publicly traded employer securities.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in regulations or in clause (ii), a plan holding employer securities which are not publicly traded employer securities shall be treated as holding publicly traded employer securities if any employer corporation, or any member of a controlled group of corporations which includes such employer corporation, has issued a class of stock which is a publicly traded employer security.

children.heading: .—Exception for certain controlled groups with publicly traded securities
children.label: ii .—Exception for certain controlled groups with publicly traded securities
I
children.text: no employer corporation, or parent corporation of an employer corporation, has issued any publicly traded employer security, and

children.label: II
children.text: no employer corporation, or parent corporation of an employer corporation, has issued any special class of stock which grants particular rights to, or bears particular risks for, the holder or issuer with respect to any corporation described in clause (i) which has issued any publicly traded employer security.

children.label: I
children.text: “controlled group of corporations” has the meaning given such term by section 1563(a), except that “50 percent” shall be substituted for “80 percent” each place it appears,

children.label: II
children.text: “employer corporation” means a corporation which is an employer maintaining the plan, and

children.label: III
children.text: “parent corporation” has the meaning given such term by section 424(e).

children.label: I
children.text: any participant in the plan, and

children.label: II
children.text: any beneficiary who has an account under the plan with respect to which the beneficiary is entitled to exercise the rights of a participant.

children.heading: .—Elective deferral
children.label: ii .—Elective deferral
children.text: The term “elective deferral” means an employer contribution described in section 402(g)(3)(A).

children.heading: .—Employer security
children.label: iii .—Employer security
children.text: The term “employer security” has the meaning given such term by section 407(d)(1) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974.

children.heading: .—Employee stock ownership plan
children.label: iv .—Employee stock ownership plan
children.text: The term “employee stock ownership plan” has the meaning given such term by section 4975(e)(7).

children.heading: .—Publicly traded employer securities
children.label: v .—Publicly traded employer securities
children.text: The term “publicly traded employer securities” means employer securities which are readily tradable on an established securities market.

children.heading: .—Year of service
children.label: vi .—Year of service
children.text: The term “year of service” has the meaning given such term by section 411(a)(5).

children.heading: Transition rule for securities attributable to employer contributions.—
children.label: H Transition rule for securities attributable to employer contributions.—

children.heading: Rules phased in over 3 years.—
children.label: i Rules phased in over 3 years.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: I .—In general
children.text: In the case of the portion of an account to which subparagraph (C) applies and which consists of employer securities acquired in a plan year beginning before , subparagraph (C) shall only apply to the applicable percentage of such securities. This subparagraph shall be applied separately with respect to each class of securities.January 1, 2007

children.heading: .—Exception for certain participants aged 55 or over
children.label: II .—Exception for certain participants aged 55 or over
children.text: Subclause (I) shall not apply to an applicable individual who is a participant who has attained age 55 and completed at least 3 years of service before the first plan year beginning after .December 31, 2005

children.heading: .—Applicable percentage
children.label: ii .—Applicable percentage
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the applicable percentage shall be determined as follows:Plan year to which subparagraph(C) applies:The applicable percentage is:1st332d663d and following100.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: A trust forming part of a pension plan shall not be treated as failing to constitute a qualified trust under this section solely because the plan provides that a distribution may be made from such trust to an employee who has attained age 59½ and who is not separated from employment at the time of such distribution.

children.heading: .—Certain employees in the building and construction industry
children.label: B .—Certain employees in the building and construction industry
i
children.text: the trust to which subparagraph (A) applies was in existence before , andJanuary 1, 1970

children.label: ii
children.text: before , at a time when the plan provided that distributions may be made to an employee who has attained age 55 and who is not separated from employment at the time of such distribution, the plan received at least 1 written determination from the Internal Revenue Service that the trust to which subparagraph (A) applies constituted a qualified trust under this section.December 31, 2011

children.heading: .—Death benefits under userra-qualified active military service
children.label: 37 .—Death benefits under userra-qualified active military service
children.text: A trust shall not constitute a qualified trust unless the plan provides that, in the case of a participant who dies while performing qualified military service (as defined in section 414(u)), the survivors of the participant are entitled to any additional benefits (other than benefit accruals relating to the period of qualified military service) provided under the plan had the participant resumed and then terminated employment on account of death.

children.label: i
children.text: qualified distributions of a lifetime income investment, or

children.label: ii
children.text: distributions of a lifetime income investment in the form of a qualified plan distribution annuity contract,

children.label: i
children.text: the term “qualified distribution” means a direct trustee-to-trustee transfer described in paragraph (31)(A) to an eligible retirement plan (as defined in section 402(c)(8)(B)),

children.label: I
children.text: which are not uniformly available with respect to other investment options under the plan, and

children.label: II
children.text: which are to a lifetime income feature available through a contract or other arrangement offered under the plan (or under another eligible retirement plan (as so defined), if paid by means of a direct trustee-to-trustee transfer described in paragraph (31)(A) to such other eligible retirement plan),

children.label: I
children.text: a feature which guarantees a minimum level of income annually (or more frequently) for at least the remainder of the life of the employee or the joint lives of the employee and the employee’s designated beneficiary, or

children.label: II
children.text: an annuity payable on behalf of the employee under which payments are made in substantially equal periodic payments (not less frequently than annually) over the life of the employee or the joint lives of the employee and the employee’s designated beneficiary, and

children.label: iv
children.text: the term “qualified plan distribution annuity contract” means an annuity contract purchased for a participant and distributed to the participant by a plan or contract described in subparagraph (B) of section 402(c)(8) (without regard to clauses (i) and (ii) thereof).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: A trust forming part of a defined contribution plan shall not be treated as failing to constitute a qualified trust under this section solely by reason of allowing qualified long-term care distributions.

children.label: I
children.text: The amount paid by or assessed to the employee during the taxable year for or with respect to certified long-term care insurance for the employee or the employee’s spouse (or other family member of the employee as provided by the Secretary by regulation).

children.label: II
children.text: An amount equal to 10 percent of the present value of the nonforfeitable accrued benefit of the employee under the plan.

children.label: I
children.text: such dollar amount, multiplied by

children.label: II
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year in which the taxable year begins, determined by substituting “calendar year 2023” for “calendar year 2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.label: i
children.text: a qualified long-term care insurance contract (as defined in section 7702B(b)) covering qualified long-term care services (as defined in section 7702B(c)),

children.label: ii
children.text: coverage of the risk that an insured individual would become a chronically ill individual (within the meaning of section 101(g)(4)(B)) under a rider or other provision of a life insurance contract which satisfies the requirements of section 101(g)(3) (determined without regard to subparagraph (D) thereof), or

children.label: iii
children.text: coverage of qualified long-term care services (as so defined) under a rider or other provision of an insurance or annuity contract which is treated as a separate contract under section 7702B(e) and satisfies the requirements of section 7702B(g),

children.heading: .—Distributions must otherwise be includible
children.label: D .—Distributions must otherwise be includible
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 402(l)(3) shall apply for purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: No distribution shall be treated as a qualified long-term care distribution unless a long-term care premium statement with respect to the employee has been filed with the plan.

children.label: I
children.text: the name and taxpayer identification number of such issuer,

children.label: II
children.text: a statement that the coverage is certified long-term care insurance,

children.label: III
children.text: identification of the employee as the owner of such coverage,

children.label: IV
children.text: identification of the individual covered and such individual’s relationship to the employee,

children.label: V
children.text: the premiums owed for the coverage for the calendar year, and

children.label: VI
children.text: such other information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: .—Filing with secretary
children.label: iii .—Filing with secretary
children.text: A long-term care premium statement will be accepted only if the issuer has completed a disclosure to the Secretary for the specific coverage product to which the statement relates. Such disclosure shall identify the issuer, type of coverage, and such other information as the Secretary may require which is included in the filing of the product with the applicable State authority.

children.heading: Certain retroactive changes in plan
children.label: 1 Certain retroactive changes in plan
children.text: A stock bonus, pension, profit-sharing, or annuity plan shall be considered as satisfying the requirements of subsection (a) for the period beginning with the date on which it was put into effect, or for the period beginning with the earlier of the date on which there was adopted or put into effect any amendment which caused the plan to fail to satisfy such requirements, and ending with the time prescribed by law for filing the return of the employer for his taxable year in which such plan or amendment was adopted (including extensions thereof) or such later time as the Secretary may designate, if all provisions of the plan which are necessary to satisfy such requirements are in effect by the end of such period and have been made effective for all purposes for the whole of such period.

children.heading: Adoption of plan
children.label: 2 Adoption of plan
children.text: If an employer adopts a stock bonus, pension, profit-sharing, or annuity plan after the close of a taxable year but before the time prescribed by law for filing the return of the employer for the taxable year (including extensions thereof), the employer may elect to treat the plan as having been adopted as of the last day of the taxable year. In the case of an individual who owns the entire interest in an unincorporated trade or business, and who is the only employee of such trade or business, any elective deferrals (as defined in section 402(g)(3)) under a qualified cash or deferred arrangement to which the preceding sentence applies, which are made by such individual before the time for filing the return of such individual for the taxable year (determined without regard to any extensions) ending after or with the end of the plan’s first plan year, shall be treated as having been made before the end of such first plan year.

children.heading: Retroactive plan amendments that increase benefit accruals
children.label: 3 Retroactive plan amendments that increase benefit accruals
A
children.text: an employer amends a stock bonus, pension, profit-sharing, or annuity plan to increase benefits accrued under the plan effective as of any date during the immediately preceding plan year (other than increasing the amount of matching contributions (as defined in subsection (m)(4)(A))),

children.label: B
children.text: such amendment would not otherwise cause the plan to fail to meet any of the requirements of this subchapter, and

children.label: C
children.text: such amendment is adopted before the time prescribed by law for filing the return of the employer for the taxable year (including extensions thereof) which includes the date described in subparagraph (A),

children.heading: Definitions and rules relating to self-employed individuals and owner-employees
children.label: c Definitions and rules relating to self-employed individuals and owner-employees

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “employee” includes, for any taxable year, an individual who is a self-employed individual for such taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: an individual who would be a self-employed individual within the meaning of the preceding sentence but for the fact that the trade or business carried on by such individual did not have net profits for the taxable year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: an individual who has been a self-employed individual within the meaning of the preceding sentence for any prior taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: only with respect to a trade or business in which personal services of the taxpayer are a material income-producing factor,

children.label: ii
children.text: without regard to paragraphs (4) and (5) of section 1402(c),

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of any individual who is treated as an employee under subparagraph (A), (C), or (D) of section 3121(d)(3), without regard to section 1402(c)(2),

children.label: iv
children.text: without regard to items which are not included in gross income for purposes of this chapter, and the deductions properly allocable to or chargeable against such items,

children.label: v
children.text: with regard to the deductions allowed by section 404 to the taxpayer, and

children.label: vi
children.text: with regard to the deduction allowed to the taxpayer by section 164(f).

children.heading: Income from disposition of certain property
children.label: C Income from disposition of certain property
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “earned income” includes gains (other than any gain which is treated under any provision of this chapter as gain from the sale or exchange of a capital asset) and net earnings derived from the sale or other disposition of, the transfer of any interest in, or the licensing of the use of property (other than good will) by an individual whose personal efforts created such property.

children.label: A
children.text: owns the entire interest in an unincorporated trade or business, or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a partnership, is a partner who owns more than 10 percent of either the capital interest or the profits interest in such partnership.

children.heading: Employer
children.label: 4 Employer
children.text: An individual who owns the entire interest in an unincorporated trade or business shall be treated as his own employer. A partnership shall be treated as the employer of each partner who is an employee within the meaning of paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: by the employer for an owner-employee, and

children.label: B
children.text: by an owner-employee as an employee.

children.heading: Special rule for certain fishermen
children.label: 6 Special rule for certain fishermen
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “self-employed individual” includes an individual described in section 3121(b)(20) (relating to certain fishermen).

children.heading: Contribution limit on owner-employees
children.label: d Contribution limit on owner-employees
children.text: A trust forming part of a pension or profit-sharing plan which provides contributions or benefits for employees some or all of whom are owner-employees shall constitute a qualified trust under this section only if, in addition to meeting the requirements of subsection (a), the plan provides that contributions on behalf of any owner-employee may be made only with respect to the earned income of such owner-employee which is derived from the trade or business with respect to which such plan is established.

children.label: e Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title VII, § 713(d)(3)98 Stat. 958July 18, 1984

children.heading: Certain custodial accounts and contracts
children.label: f Certain custodial accounts and contracts

children.text: the custodial account or contract would, except for the fact that it is not a trust, constitute a qualified trust under this section, and

in the case of a custodial account the assets thereof are held by a bank (as defined in section 408(n)) or another person who demonstrates, to the satisfaction of the Secretary, that the manner in which he will hold the assets will be consistent with the requirements of this section.

children.heading: Annuity defined
children.label: g Annuity defined
children.text: For purposes of this section and sections 402, 403, and 404, the term “annuity” includes a face-amount certificate, as defined in section 2(a)(15) of the Investment Company Act of 1940 (15 U.S.C., sec. 80a–2); but does not include any contract or certificate issued after , which is transferable, if any person other than the trustee of a trust described in section 401(a) which is exempt from tax under section 501(a) is the owner of such contract or certificate.December 31, 1962

children.heading: Medical, etc., benefits for retired employees and their spouses and dependents
children.label: h Medical, etc., benefits for retired employees and their spouses and dependents

children.text: such benefits are subordinate to the retirement benefits provided by the plan,
a separate account is established and maintained for such benefits,
the employer’s contributions to such separate account are reasonable and ascertainable,

it is impossible, at any time prior to the satisfaction of all liabilities under the plan to provide such benefits, for any part of the corpus or income of such separate account to be (within the taxable year or thereafter) used for, or diverted to, any purpose other than the providing of such benefits,

notwithstanding the provisions of subsection (a)(2), upon the satisfaction of all liabilities under the plan to provide such benefits, any amount remaining in such separate account must, under the terms of the plan, be returned to the employer, and

in the case of an employee who is a key employee, a separate account is established and maintained for such benefits payable to such employee (and his spouse and dependents) and such benefits (to the extent attributable to plan years beginning after , for which the employee is a key employee) are only payable to such employee (and his spouse and dependents) from such separate account.March 31, 1984

children.heading: Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 97–248, title II, § 238(b)96 Stat. 512Sept. 3, 1982
children.label: j Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 97–248, title II, § 238(b)96 Stat. 512Sept. 3, 1982

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: A profit-sharing or stock bonus plan, a pre-ERISA money purchase plan, or a rural cooperative plan shall not be considered as not satisfying the requirements of subsection (a) merely because the plan includes a qualified cash or deferred arrangement.

children.label: A
children.text: under which a covered employee may elect to have the employer make payments as contributions to a trust under the plan on behalf of the employee, or to the employee directly in cash;

children.label: I
children.text: severance from employment, death, or disability,

children.label: II
children.text: an event described in paragraph (10),

children.label: III
children.text: in the case of a profit-sharing or stock bonus plan, the attainment of age 59½,

children.label: IV
children.text: subject to the provisions of paragraph (14), upon hardship of the employee,

children.label: V
children.text: in the case of a qualified reservist distribution (as defined in section 72(t)(2)(G)(iii)), the date on which a period referred to in subclause (III) of such section begins,

children.label: VI
children.text: except as may be otherwise provided by regulations, with respect to amounts invested in a lifetime income investment (as defined in subsection (a)(38)(B)(ii)), the date that is 90 days prior to the date that such lifetime income investment may no longer be held as an investment option under the arrangement, or

children.label: VII
children.text: as provided in section 401(a)(39),

children.label: ii

children.label: C

children.label: II

children.heading: .—Special rule for early participation
children.label: F .—Special rule for early participation
children.text: If an employer elects to apply section 410(b)(4)(B) in determining whether a cash or deferred arrangement meets the requirements of subparagraph (A)(i), the employer may, in determining whether the arrangement meets the requirements of subparagraph (A)(ii), exclude from consideration all eligible employees (other than highly compensated employees) who have not met the minimum age and service requirements of section 410(a)(1)(A).

children.heading: .—Governmental plan
children.label: G .—Governmental plan
children.text: A governmental plan (within the meaning of section 414(d)) shall be treated as meeting the requirements of this paragraph.

children.heading: Benefits (other than matching contributions) must not be contingent on election to defer
children.label: A Benefits (other than matching contributions) must not be contingent on election to defer
children.text: A cash or deferred arrangement of any employer shall not be treated as a qualified cash or deferred arrangement if any other benefit (other than a de minimis financial incentive (not paid for with plan assets) provided to employees who elect to have the employer make contributions under the arrangement in lieu of receiving cash) is conditioned (directly or indirectly) on the employee electing to have the employer make or not make contributions under the arrangement in lieu of receiving cash. The preceding sentence shall not apply to any matching contribution (as defined in section 401(m)) made by reason of such an election.

children.heading: Eligibility of State and local governments and tax-exempt organizations
children.label: B Eligibility of State and local governments and tax-exempt organizations

children.heading: Tax-exempts eligible
children.label: i Tax-exempts eligible
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), any organization exempt from tax under this subtitle may include a qualified cash or deferred arrangement as part of a plan maintained by it.

children.heading: Governments ineligible
children.label: ii Governments ineligible
children.text: A cash or deferred arrangement shall not be treated as a qualified cash or deferred arrangement if it is part of a plan maintained by a State or local government or political subdivision thereof, or any agency or instrumentality thereof. This clause shall not apply to a rural cooperative plan or to a plan of an employer described in clause (iii).

children.heading: Treatment of Indian tribal governments
children.label: iii Treatment of Indian tribal governments
children.text: An employer which is an Indian tribal government (as defined in section 7701(a)(40)), a subdivision of an Indian tribal government (determined in accordance with section 7871(d)), an agency or instrumentality of an Indian tribal government or subdivision thereof, or a corporation chartered under Federal, State, or tribal law which is owned in whole or in part by any of the foregoing may include a qualified cash or deferred arrangement as part of a plan maintained by the employer.

children.heading: Coordination with other plans
children.label: C Coordination with other plans
children.text: Except as provided in section 401(m), any employer contribution made pursuant to an employee’s election under a qualified cash or deferred arrangement shall not be taken into account for purposes of determining whether any other plan meets the requirements of section 401(a) or 410(b). This subparagraph shall not apply for purposes of determining whether a plan meets the average benefit requirement of section 410(b)(2)(A)(ii).

children.heading: Highly compensated employee
children.label: 5 Highly compensated employee
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “highly compensated employee” has the meaning given such term by section 414(q).

children.label: A
children.text: which is a defined contribution plan (as defined in section 414(i)),

children.label: B
children.text: which was in existence on , and which, on such date, included a salary reduction arrangement, andJune 27, 1974

children.label: C
children.text: under which neither the employee contributions nor the employer contributions may exceed the levels provided for by the contribution formula in effect under the plan on such date.

children.label: ii

children.label: I

children.label: II
children.text: is engaged primarily in providing electric service to the public in its area of service and which is exempt from tax under this subtitle or which is a State or local government (or an agency or instrumentality thereof), other than a municipality (or an agency or instrumentality thereof),

children.label: ii
children.text: any organization described in paragraph (4) or (6) of section 501(c) and at least 80 percent of the members of which are organizations described in clause (i),

children.label: iii
children.text: a cooperative telephone company described in section 501(c)(12),

children.label: I
children.text: is a mutual irrigation or ditch company described in section 501(c)(12) (without regard to the 85 percent requirement thereof), or

children.label: II
children.text: is a district organized under the laws of a State as a municipal corporation for the purpose of irrigation, water conservation, or drainage, and

children.label: v
children.text: an organization which is a national association of organizations described in clause (i), (ii),, (iii), or (iv).2So in original.2

children.heading: Special rule for certain distributions
children.label: C Special rule for certain distributions
children.text: A rural cooperative plan which includes a qualified cash or deferred arrangement shall not be treated as violating the requirements of section 401(a) or of paragraph (2) merely by reason of a hardship distribution or a distribution to a participant after attainment of age 59½. For purposes of this section, the term “hardship distribution” means a distribution described in paragraph (2)(B)(i)(IV) (without regard to the limitation of its application to profit-sharing or stock bonus plans).

children.heading: Arrangement not disqualified if excess contributions distributed
children.label: 8 Arrangement not disqualified if excess contributions distributed
i
children.text: the amount of the excess contributions for such plan year (and any income allocable to such contributions through the end of such year) is distributed, or

children.label: ii
children.text: to the extent provided in regulations, the employee elects to treat the amount of the excess contributions as an amount distributed to the employee and then contributed by the employee to the plan.

children.label: i
children.text: the aggregate amount of employer contributions actually paid over to the trust on behalf of highly compensated employees for such plan year, over

children.label: ii
children.text: the maximum amount of such contributions permitted under the limitations of clause (ii) of paragraph (3)(A) (determined by reducing contributions made on behalf of highly compensated employees in order of the actual deferral percentages beginning with the highest of such percentages).

children.heading: Method of distributing excess contributions
children.label: C Method of distributing excess contributions
children.text: Any distribution of the excess contributions for any plan year shall be made to highly compensated employees on the basis of the amount of contributions by, or on behalf of, each of such employees.

children.heading: Additional tax under section 72(t) not to apply
children.label: D Additional tax under section 72(t) not to apply
children.text: No tax shall be imposed under section 72(t) on any amount required to be distributed under this paragraph.

children.heading: Treatment of matching contributions forfeited by reason of excess deferral or contribution or permissible withdrawal
children.label: E Treatment of matching contributions forfeited by reason of excess deferral or contribution or permissible withdrawal
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (2)(C), a matching contribution (within the meaning of subsection (m)) shall not be treated as forfeitable merely because such contribution is forfeitable if the contribution to which the matching contribution relates is treated as an excess contribution under subparagraph (B), an excess deferral under section 402(g)(2)(A), a permissible withdrawal under section 414(w), or an excess aggregate contribution under section 401(m)(6)(B).

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: F Cross reference
children.text: For excise tax on certain excess contributions, see section 4979.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: 9 Compensation
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “compensation” has the meaning given such term by section 414(s).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An event described in this subparagraph is the termination of the plan without establishment or maintenance of another defined contribution plan (other than an employee stock ownership plan as defined in section 4975(e)(7)).

children.heading: Distributions must be lump sum distributions
children.label: B Distributions must be lump sum distributions

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A termination shall not be treated as described in subparagraph (A) with respect to any employee unless the employee receives a lump sum distribution by reason of the termination.

children.label: I
children.text: a trust which forms a part of a plan described in section 401(a) and which is exempt from tax under section 501(a), or

children.label: II
children.text: an annuity plan described in section 403(a).

children.heading: Adoption of simple plan to meet nondiscrimination tests
children.label: 11 Adoption of simple plan to meet nondiscrimination tests
i
children.text: the contribution requirements of subparagraph (B),

children.label: ii
children.text: the exclusive plan requirements of subparagraph (C), and

children.label: iii
children.text: the vesting requirements of section 408(p)(3).

children.label: I
children.text: an employee may elect to have the employer make elective contributions for the year on behalf of the employee to a trust under the plan in an amount which is expressed as a percentage of compensation of the employee but which in no event exceeds the amount in effect under section 408(p)(2)(A)(ii) (after the application of any election under section 408(p)(2)(E)(i)(II)),

children.label: II
children.text: the employer is required to make a matching contribution to the trust for the year in an amount equal to so much of the amount the employee elects under subclause (I) as does not exceed 3 percent of compensation for the year,

children.label: III
children.text: the employer may make nonelective contributions of a uniform percentage (up to 10 percent) of compensation, but not to exceed the amount in effect under section 408(p)(2)(A)(iv) in any year, for each employee who is eligible to participate in the arrangement and who has at least $5,000 of compensation from the employer for the year, and

children.label: IV
children.text: no other contributions may be made other than contributions described in subclause (I), (II), or (III).

children.heading: Employer may elect 2-percent nonelective contribution
children.label: ii Employer may elect 2-percent nonelective contribution
children.text: An employer shall be treated as meeting the requirements of clause (i)(II) for any year if, in lieu of the contributions described in such clause, the employer elects (pursuant to the terms of the arrangement) to make nonelective contributions of 2 percent of compensation for each employee who is eligible to participate in the arrangement and who has at least $5,000 of compensation from the employer for the year. If an employer makes an election under this subparagraph for any year, the employer shall notify employees of such election within a reasonable period of time before the 60th day before the beginning of such year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of subparagraphs (B) and (C) of section 408(p)(5) shall apply for purposes of this subparagraph.

children.heading: Notice of election period
children.label: II Notice of election period
children.text: The requirements of this subparagraph shall not be treated as met with respect to any year unless the employer notifies each employee eligible to participate, within a reasonable period of time before the 60th day before the beginning of such year (and, for the first year the employee is so eligible, the 60th day before the first day such employee is so eligible), of the rules similar to the rules of section 408(p)(5)(C) which apply by reason of subclause (I).

children.heading: Exclusive plan requirement
children.label: C Exclusive plan requirement
children.text: The requirements of this subparagraph are met for any year to which this paragraph applies if no contributions were made, or benefits were accrued, for services during such year under any qualified plan of the employer on behalf of any employee eligible to participate in the cash or deferred arrangement, other than contributions described in subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: i Definitions
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, any term used in this paragraph which is also used in section 408(p) shall have the meaning given such term by such section.

children.heading: Coordination with top-heavy rules
children.label: ii Coordination with top-heavy rules
children.text: A plan meeting the requirements of this paragraph for any year shall not be treated as a top-heavy plan under section 416 for such year if such plan allows only contributions required under this paragraph.

children.heading: Employers electing increased contributions
children.label: E Employers electing increased contributions
children.text: In the case of an employer which applies an election under section 408(p)(2)(E)(i)(II) for purposes of the contribution requirements of this paragraph under subparagraph (B)(i)(I), rules similar to the rules of subparagraphs (B)(iii), (C)(ii)(IV), and (G) of section 408(p)(2) shall apply for purposes of subparagraphs (B)(i)(II) and (B)(ii) of this paragraph.

children.label: 12 Alternative methods of meeting nondiscrimination requirements
i

children.heading: Rate for highly compensated employees
children.label: ii Rate for highly compensated employees
children.text: The requirements of this subparagraph are not met if, under the arrangement, the rate of matching contribution with respect to any elective contribution of a highly compensated employee at any rate of elective contribution is greater than that with respect to an employee who is not a highly compensated employee.

children.text: the rate of an employer’s matching contribution does not increase as an employee’s rate of elective contributions increase, and

children.label: II
children.text: the aggregate amount of matching contributions at such rate of elective contribution is at least equal to the aggregate amount of matching contributions which would be made if matching contributions were made on the basis of the percentages described in clause (i).

children.heading: Nonelective contributions
children.label: C Nonelective contributions
children.text: The requirements of this subparagraph are met if, under the arrangement, the employer is required, without regard to whether the employee makes an elective contribution or employee contribution, to make a contribution to a defined contribution plan on behalf of each employee who is not a highly compensated employee and who is eligible to participate in the arrangement in an amount equal to at least 3 percent of the employee’s compensation.

children.label: ii

children.heading: Withdrawal and vesting restrictions
children.label: i Withdrawal and vesting restrictions
children.text: An arrangement shall not be treated as meeting the requirements of subparagraph (B) or (C) of this paragraph unless the requirements of subparagraphs (B) and (C) of paragraph (2) are met with respect to all employer contributions (including matching contributions) taken into account in determining whether the requirements of subparagraphs (B) and (C) of this paragraph are met.

children.heading: Social security and similar contributions not taken into account
children.label: ii Social security and similar contributions not taken into account
children.text: An arrangement shall not be treated as meeting the requirements of subparagraph (B) or (C) unless such requirements are met without regard to subsection (), and, for purposes of subsection (), employer contributions under subparagraph (B) or (C) shall not be taken into account.ll

I

children.label: II

children.heading: Exception where plan provided for matching contributions
children.label: ii Exception where plan provided for matching contributions
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to any plan year if the plan provided at any time during the plan year that the requirements of subparagraph (B) or paragraph (13)(D)(i)(I) applied to the plan year.

children.heading: 4-percent contribution requirement
children.label: iii 4-percent contribution requirement
children.text: Clause (i)(II) shall not apply to an arrangement unless the amount of the contributions described in subparagraph (C) which the employer is required to make under the arrangement for the plan year with respect to any employee is an amount equal to at least 4 percent of the employee’s compensation.

children.heading: Other plans
children.label: G Other plans
children.text: An arrangement shall be treated as meeting the contribution requirements under subparagraph (B) or (C) if any other plan maintained by the employer meets such requirements with respect to employees eligible under the arrangement.

children.heading: Alternative method for automatic contribution arrangements to meet nondiscrimination requirements
children.label: 13 Alternative method for automatic contribution arrangements to meet nondiscrimination requirements

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A qualified automatic contribution arrangement shall be treated as meeting the requirements of paragraph (3)(A)(ii).

children.label: i

children.label: ii
children.text: which is described in subparagraph (D)(i)(II) and meets the applicable requirements of subparagraphs (C) and (D).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The requirements of this subparagraph are met if, under the arrangement, each employee eligible to participate in the arrangement is treated as having elected to have the employer make elective contributions in an amount equal to a qualified percentage of compensation.

children.label: I

children.label: II

children.label: III

children.label: IV
children.text: 6 percent during any subsequent plan year.

children.label: iv Automatic deferral for current employees not required
I
children.text: was eligible to participate in the arrangement (or a predecessor arrangement) immediately before the date on which such arrangement becomes a qualified automatic contribution arrangement (determined after application of this clause), and

children.label: II

children.label: II
children.text: is required, without regard to whether the employee makes an elective contribution or employee contribution, to make a contribution to a defined contribution plan on behalf of each employee who is not a highly compensated employee and who is eligible to participate in the arrangement in an amount equal to at least 3 percent of the employee’s compensation.

children.heading: Application of rules for matching contributions
children.label: ii Application of rules for matching contributions
children.text: The rules of clauses (ii) and (iii) of paragraph (12)(B) shall apply for purposes of clause (i)(I).

children.heading: Application of certain other rules
children.label: iv Application of certain other rules
children.text: The rules of subparagraphs (E)(ii) and (G) of paragraph (12) shall apply for purposes of subclauses (I) and (II) of clause (i).

children.label: I

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of an arrangement under which the employee may elect among 2 or more investment options, the notice explains how contributions made under the arrangement will be invested in the absence of any investment election by the employee, and

children.label: III
children.text: the employee has a reasonable period of time after receipt of the notice described in subclauses (I) and (II) and before the first elective contribution is made to make either such election.

children.heading: Timing of plan amendment for employer making nonelective contributions
children.label: F Timing of plan amendment for employer making nonelective contributions
I
children.text: at any time before the 30th day before the close of the plan year, or

children.label: II
children.text: at any time before the last day under paragraph (8)(A) for distributing excess contributions for the plan year.

children.heading: Exception where plan provided for matching contributions
children.label: ii Exception where plan provided for matching contributions
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to any plan year if the plan provided at any time during the plan year that the requirements of subparagraph (D)(i)(I) or paragraph (12)(B) applied to the plan year.

children.heading: 4-percent contribution requirement
children.label: iii 4-percent contribution requirement
children.text: Clause (i)(II) shall not apply to an arrangement unless the amount of the contributions described in subparagraph (D)(i)(II) which the employer is required to make under the arrangement for the plan year with respect to any employee is an amount equal to at least 4 percent of the employee’s compensation.

children.heading: Special rules relating to hardship withdrawals
children.label: 14 Special rules relating to hardship withdrawals

children.heading: Amounts which may be withdrawn
children.label: A Amounts which may be withdrawn
i
children.text: Contributions to a profit-sharing or stock bonus plan to which section 402(e)(3) applies.

children.label: ii
children.text: Qualified nonelective contributions (as defined in subsection (m)(4)(C)).

children.label: iii
children.text: Qualified matching contributions described in paragraph (3)(D)(ii)(I).

children.label: iv
children.text: Earnings on any contributions described in clause (i), (ii), or (iii).

children.heading: No requirement to take available loan
children.label: B No requirement to take available loan
children.text: A distribution shall not be treated as failing to be made upon the hardship of an employee solely because the employee does not take any available loan under the plan.

children.label: i
