children.label: ii
children.text: not in excess of the amount required to satisfy such financial need, and

children.heading: Special rules for participation requirement for long-term, part-time workers
children.label: 15 Special rules for participation requirement for long-term, part-time workers

children.heading: Age requirement must be met
children.label: A Age requirement must be met
children.text: Paragraph (2)(D)(ii) shall not apply to an employee unless the employee has met the requirement of section 410(a)(1)(A)(i) by the close of the last of the 12-month periods described in such paragraph.

children.heading: Nondiscrimination and top-heavy rules not to apply
children.label: B Nondiscrimination and top-heavy rules not to apply
I
children.text: notwithstanding subsection (a)(4), an employer shall not be required to make nonelective or matching contributions on behalf of such employees even if such contributions are made on behalf of other employees eligible to participate in the arrangement, and

children.label: II
children.text: an employer may elect to exclude such employees from the application of subsection (a)(4), paragraphs (3), (12), and (13), paragraphs (2), (11), and (12) of subsection (m), and section 410(b).

children.heading: Top-heavy rules
children.label: ii Top-heavy rules
children.text: An employer may elect to exclude all employees who are eligible to participate in a plan maintained by the employer solely by reason of paragraph (2)(D)(ii) from the application of the vesting and benefit requirements under subsections (b) and (c) of section 416.

children.heading: Vesting
children.label: iii Vesting
children.text: For purposes of determining whether an employee described in clause (i) has a nonforfeitable right to employer contributions (other than contributions described in paragraph (3)(D)(i)) under the plan, each 12-month period for which the employee has at least 500 hours of service shall be treated as a year of service, and section 411(a)(6) shall be applied by substituting “at least 500 hours of service” for “more than 500 hours of service” in subparagraph (A) thereof.

children.heading: Employees who become full-time employees
children.label: iv Employees who become full-time employees
children.text: This subparagraph (other than clause (iii)) shall cease to apply to any employee as of the first plan year beginning after the plan year in which the employee meets the requirements of paragraph (2)(D) without regard to paragraph (2)(D)(ii).

children.heading: Exception for employees under collectively bargained plans, etc.
children.label: C Exception for employees under collectively bargained plans, etc.
children.text: Paragraph (2)(D)(ii) shall not apply to employees described in section 410(b)(3).

children.heading: Time of participation
children.label: i Time of participation
children.text: The rules of section 410(a)(4) shall apply to an employee eligible to participate in an arrangement solely by reason of paragraph (2)(D)(ii).

children.heading: 12-month periods
children.label: ii 12-month periods
children.text: 12-month periods shall be determined in the same manner as under the last sentence of section 410(a)(3)(A).

children.heading: Starter 401(k) deferral-only plans for employers with no retirement plan
children.label: 16 Starter 401(k) deferral-only plans for employers with no retirement plan

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A starter 401(k) deferral-only arrangement maintained by an eligible employer shall be treated as meeting the requirements of paragraph (3)(A)(ii).

children.label: i
children.text: the automatic deferral requirements of subparagraph (C),

children.label: ii
children.text: the contribution limitations of subparagraph (D), and

children.label: iii
children.text: the requirements of subparagraph (E) of paragraph (13).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The requirements of this subparagraph are met if, under the arrangement, each eligible employee is treated as having elected to have the employer make elective contributions in an amount equal to a qualified percentage of compensation.

children.label: I
children.text: to not have such contributions made, or

children.label: II
children.text: to make elective contributions at a level specified in such affirmative election.

children.heading: Qualified percentage
children.label: iii Qualified percentage
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “qualified percentage” means, with respect to any employee, any percentage determined under the arrangement if such percentage is applied uniformly and is not less than 3 or more than 15 percent.

children.label: I
children.text: the only contributions which may be made are elective contributions of employees described in subparagraph (C), and

children.label: II
children.text: the aggregate amount of such elective contributions which may be made with respect to any employee for any calendar year shall not exceed $6,000.

children.heading: Cost-of-living adjustment
children.label: ii Cost-of-living adjustment
children.text: In the case of any calendar year beginning after , the $6,000 amount under clause (i) shall be adjusted in the same manner as under section 402(g)(4), except that “2023” shall be substituted for “2005”.December 31, 2024

children.heading: Catch-up contributions for individuals age 50 or over
children.label: iii Catch-up contributions for individuals age 50 or over
children.text: In the case of an individual who has attained the age of 50 before the close of the taxable year, the limitation under clause (i)(II) shall be increased by the applicable amount determined under section 219(b)(5)(B)(ii) (after the application of section 219(b)(5)(C)(iii)).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “eligible employer” means any employer if the employer does not maintain a qualified plan with respect to which contributions are made, or benefits are accrued, for service in the year for which the determination is being made. If only individuals other than employees described in subparagraph (A) of section 410(b)(3) are eligible to participate in such arrangement, then the preceding sentence shall be applied without regard to any qualified plan in which only employees described in such subparagraph are eligible to participate.

children.heading: Relief for acquisitions, etc.
children.label: ii Relief for acquisitions, etc.
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 408(p)(10) shall apply for purposes of clause (i).

children.heading: Qualified plan
children.label: iii Qualified plan
children.text: The term “qualified plan” means a plan, contract, pension, account, or trust described in subparagraph (A) or (B) of paragraph (5) of section 219(g) (determined without regard to the last sentence of such paragraph (5)).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “eligible employee” means any employee of the employer who meets the minimum age and service conditions described in section 410(a)(1).

children.heading: Exclusions
children.label: ii Exclusions
children.text: The employer may elect to exclude from such definition any employee described in paragraph (3) or (4) of section 410(b).

children.heading: Permitted disparity in plan contributions or benefits
children.label: l Permitted disparity in plan contributions or benefits
A
children.text: in the case of a defined contribution plan, the requirements of paragraph (2) are met, and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a defined benefit plan, the requirements of paragraph (3) are met.

children.label: i
children.text: the base contribution percentage, or

children.label: I
children.text: 5.7 percentage points, or

children.label: II
children.text: the percentage equal to the portion of the rate of tax under section 3111(a) (in effect as of the beginning of the year) which is attributable to old-age insurance.

children.heading: Excess contribution percentage
children.label: i Excess contribution percentage
children.text: The term “excess contribution percentage” means the percentage of compensation which is contributed by the employer under the plan with respect to that portion of each participant’s compensation in excess of the integration level.

children.heading: Base contribution percentage
children.label: ii Base contribution percentage
children.text: The term “base contribution percentage” means the percentage of compensation contributed by the employer under the plan with respect to that portion of each participant’s compensation not in excess of the integration level.

children.label: I
children.text: the excess benefit percentage does not exceed the base benefit percentage by more than the maximum excess allowance,

children.label: II
children.text: any optional form of benefit, preretirement benefit, actuarial factor, or other benefit or feature provided with respect to compensation in excess of the integration level is provided with respect to compensation not in excess of such level, and

children.label: III
children.text: benefits are based on average annual compensation.

children.heading: Benefit percentages
children.label: ii Benefit percentages
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the excess and base benefit percentages shall be computed in the same manner as the excess and base contribution percentages under paragraph (2)(B), except that such determination shall be made on the basis of benefits attributable to employer contributions rather than contributions.

children.label: i
children.text: a participant’s accrued benefit attributable to employer contributions (within the meaning of section 411(c)(1)) may not be reduced (by reason of the offset) by more than the maximum offset allowance, and

children.label: ii
children.text: benefits are based on average annual compensation.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of benefits attributable to any year of service with the employer taken into account under the plan, ¾ of a percentage point, and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of total benefits, ¾ of a percentage point, multiplied by the participant’s years of service (not in excess of 35) with the employer taken into account under the plan.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of benefits attributable to any year of service with the employer taken into account under the plan, ¾ percent of the participant’s final average compensation, and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of total benefits, ¾ percent of the participant’s final average compensation, multiplied by the participant’s years of service (not in excess of 35) with the employer taken into account under the plan.

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a plan other than an offset plan which has an integration level in excess of covered compensation, or

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to any participant in an offset plan who has final average compensation in excess of covered compensation.

children.heading: Basis of reductions
children.label: ii Basis of reductions
children.text: Any reductions under clause (i) shall be based on the percentages of compensation replaced by the employer-derived portions of primary insurance amounts under the Social Security Act for participants with compensation in excess of covered compensation.

children.heading: Offset plan
children.label: D Offset plan
children.text: The term “offset plan” means any plan with respect to which the benefit attributable to employer contributions for each participant is reduced by an amount specified in the plan.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “integration level” means the amount of compensation specified under the plan (by dollar amount or formula) at or below which the rate at which contributions or benefits are provided (expressed as a percentage) is less than such rate above such amount.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: The integration level for any year may not exceed the contribution and benefit base in effect under section 230 of the Social Security Act for such year.

children.heading: Level to apply to all participants
children.label: iii Level to apply to all participants
children.text: A plan’s integration level shall apply with respect to all participants in the plan.

children.heading: Multiple integration levels
children.label: iv Multiple integration levels
children.text: Under rules prescribed by the Secretary, a defined benefit plan may specify multiple integration levels.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: B Compensation
children.text: The term “compensation” has the meaning given such term by section 414(s).

children.label: i
children.text: any period of at least 3 consecutive years, or

children.label: ii
children.text: if shorter, the participant’s full period of service.

children.label: I
children.text: the 3-consecutive year period ending with the current year, or

children.label: II
children.text: if shorter, the participant’s full period of service.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: A participant’s final average compensation shall be determined by not taking into account in any year compensation in excess of the contribution and benefit base in effect under section 230 of the Social Security Act for such year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “covered compensation” means, with respect to an employee, the average of the contribution and benefit bases in effect under section 230 of the Social Security Act for each year in the 35-year period ending with the year in which the employee attains the social security retirement age.

children.heading: Computation for any year
children.label: ii Computation for any year
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the determination for any year preceding the year in which the employee attains the social security retirement age shall be made by assuming that there is no increase in the bases described in clause (i) after the determination year and before the employee attains the social security retirement age.

children.heading: Social security retirement age
children.label: iii Social security retirement age
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “social security retirement age” has the meaning given such term by section 415(b)(8).

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a defined benefit plan which provides for unreduced benefits commencing before the social security retirement age (as defined in section 415(b)(8)), rules providing for the reduction of the maximum excess allowance and the maximum offset allowance, and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of an employee covered by 2 or more plans of the employer which fail to meet the requirements of subsection (a)(4) (without regard to this subsection), rules preventing the multiple use of the disparity permitted under this subsection with respect to any employee.

children.heading: Special rule for plan maintained by railroads
children.label: 6 Special rule for plan maintained by railroads
children.text: In determining whether a plan which includes employees of a railroad employer who are entitled to benefits under the Railroad Retirement Act of 1974 meets the requirements of this subsection, rules similar to the rules set forth in this subsection shall apply. Such rules shall take into account the employer-derived portion of the employees’ tier 2 railroad retirement benefits and any supplemental annuity under the Railroad Retirement Act of 1974.

children.heading: Nondiscrimination test for matching contributions and employee contributions
children.label: m Nondiscrimination test for matching contributions and employee contributions

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A defined contribution plan shall be treated as meeting the requirements of subsection (a)(4) with respect to the amount of any matching contribution or employee contribution for any plan year only if the contribution percentage requirement of paragraph (2) of this subsection is met for such plan year.

children.label: i
children.text: 125 percent of such percentage for all other eligible employees for the preceding plan year, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the lesser of 200 percent of such percentage for all other eligible employees for the preceding plan year, or such percentage for all other eligible employees for the preceding plan year plus 2 percentage points.

children.heading: Multiple plans treated as a single plan
children.label: B Multiple plans treated as a single plan
children.text: If two or more plans of an employer to which matching contributions, employee contributions, or elective deferrals are made are treated as one plan for purposes of section 410(b), such plans shall be treated as one plan for purposes of this subsection. If a highly compensated employee participates in two or more plans of an employer to which contributions to which this subsection applies are made, all such contributions shall be aggregated for purposes of this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: the sum of the matching contributions and employee contributions paid under the plan on behalf of each such employee for such plan year, to

children.label: B
children.text: the employee’s compensation (within the meaning of section 414(s)) for such plan year.

children.label: i
children.text: any employer contribution made to a defined contribution plan on behalf of an employee on account of an employee contribution made by such employee,

children.label: ii
children.text: any employer contribution made to a defined contribution plan on behalf of an employee on account of an employee’s elective deferral, and

children.label: iii
children.text: subject to the requirements of paragraph (14), any employer contribution made to a defined contribution plan on behalf of an employee on account of a qualified student loan payment.

children.heading: Elective deferral
children.label: B Elective deferral
children.text: The term “elective deferral” means any employer contribution described in section 402(g)(3).

children.label: i
children.text: the employee may not elect to have the contribution paid to the employee in cash instead of being contributed to the plan, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the requirements of subparagraphs (B) and (C) of subsection (k)(2) are met.

children.label: I
children.text: the limitation applicable under section 402(g) for the year (or, if lesser, the employee’s compensation (as defined in section 415(c)(3)) for the year), reduced by

children.label: II
children.text: the elective deferrals made by the employee for such year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: if the employee certifies annually to the employer making the matching contribution under this paragraph that such payment has been made on such loan.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any employee who is eligible to make an employee contribution (or, if the employer takes elective contributions into account, elective contributions) or to receive a matching contribution under the plan being tested under paragraph (1) shall be considered an eligible employee for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Certain nonparticipants
children.label: B Certain nonparticipants
children.text: If an employee contribution is required as a condition of participation in the plan, any employee who would be a participant in the plan if such employee made such a contribution shall be treated as an eligible employee on behalf of whom no employer contributions are made.

children.heading: Special rule for early participation
children.label: C Special rule for early participation
children.text: If an employer elects to apply section 410(b)(4)(B) in determining whether a plan meets the requirements of section 410(b), the employer may, in determining whether the plan meets the requirements of paragraph (2), exclude from consideration all eligible employees (other than highly compensated employees) who have not met the minimum age and service requirements of section 410(a)(1)(A).

children.heading: Plan not disqualified if excess aggregate contributions distributed before end of following plan year
children.label: 6 Plan not disqualified if excess aggregate contributions distributed before end of following plan year

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A plan shall not be treated as failing to meet the requirements of paragraph (1) for any plan year if, before the close of the following plan year, the amount of the excess aggregate contributions for such plan year (and any income allocable to such contributions through the end of such year) is distributed (or, if forfeitable, is forfeited). Such contributions (and such income) may be distributed without regard to any other provision of law.

children.label: i
children.text: the aggregate amount of the matching contributions and employee contributions (and any qualified nonelective contribution or elective contribution taken into account in computing the contribution percentage) actually made on behalf of highly compensated employees for such plan year, over

children.label: ii
children.text: the maximum amount of such contributions permitted under the limitations of paragraph (2)(A) (determined by reducing contributions made on behalf of highly compensated employees in order of their contribution percentages beginning with the highest of such percentages).

children.heading: Method of distributing excess aggregate contributions
children.label: C Method of distributing excess aggregate contributions
children.text: Any distribution of the excess aggregate contributions for any plan year shall be made to highly compensated employees on the basis of the amount of contributions on behalf of, or by, each such employee. Forfeitures of excess aggregate contributions may not be allocated to participants whose contributions are reduced under this paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: first determining the excess deferrals (within the meaning of section 402(g)), and

children.label: ii
children.text: then determining the excess contributions under subsection (k).

children.heading: Additional tax of section 72(t) not applicable
children.label: A Additional tax of section 72(t) not applicable
children.text: No tax shall be imposed under section 72(t) on any amount required to be distributed under paragraph (6).

children.heading: Exclusion of employee contributions
children.label: B Exclusion of employee contributions
children.text: Any distribution attributable to employee contributions shall not be included in gross income except to the extent attributable to income on such contributions.

children.heading: Highly compensated employee
children.label: 8 Highly compensated employee
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “highly compensated employee” has the meaning given to such term by section 414(q).

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 9 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this subsection and subsection (k), including regulations permitting appropriate aggregation of plans and contributions.

children.label: A
children.text: meets the contribution requirements of subparagraph (B) of subsection (k)(11),

children.label: B
children.text: meets the exclusive plan requirements of subsection (k)(11)(C), and

children.label: C
children.text: meets the vesting requirements of section 408(p)(3).

children.heading: Additional alternative method of satisfying tests
children.label: 11 Additional alternative method of satisfying tests
i
children.text: meets the contribution requirements of subparagraph (B) or (C) of subsection (k)(12),

children.label: ii
children.text: meets the notice requirements of subsection (k)(12)(D), and

children.label: iii
children.text: meets the requirements of subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: matching contributions on behalf of any employee may not be made with respect to an employee’s contributions or elective deferrals in excess of 6 percent of the employee’s compensation,

children.label: ii
children.text: the rate of an employer’s matching contribution does not increase as the rate of an employee’s contributions or elective deferrals increase, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the matching contribution with respect to any highly compensated employee at any rate of an employee contribution or rate of elective deferral is not greater than that with respect to an employee who is not a highly compensated employee.

children.heading: Alternative method for automatic contribution arrangements
children.label: 12 Alternative method for automatic contribution arrangements
A
children.text: is a qualified automatic contribution arrangement (as defined in subsection (k)(13)),

children.label: B
children.text: meets the notice requirements of subsection (k)(13)(E), and

children.label: C
children.text: meets the requirements of paragraph (11)(B).

children.heading: Matching contributions for qualified student loan payments
children.label: 13 Matching contributions for qualified student loan payments
i
children.text: the plan provides matching contributions on account of elective deferrals at the same rate as contributions on account of qualified student loan payments,

children.label: ii
children.text: the plan provides matching contributions on account of qualified student loan payments only on behalf of employees otherwise eligible to receive matching contributions on account of elective deferrals,

children.label: iii
children.text: under the plan, all employees eligible to receive matching contributions on account of elective deferrals are eligible to receive matching contributions on account of qualified student loan payments, and

children.label: iv
children.text: the plan provides that matching contributions on account of qualified student loan payments vest in the same manner as matching contributions on account of elective deferrals.

children.heading: Treatment for purposes of nondiscrimination rules, etc.
children.label: B Treatment for purposes of nondiscrimination rules, etc.

children.heading: Nondiscrimination rules
children.label: i Nondiscrimination rules
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A)(iii), subsection (a)(4), and section 410(b), matching contributions described in paragraph (4)(A)(iii) shall not fail to be treated as available to an employee solely because such employee does not have debt incurred under a qualified education loan (as defined in section 221(d)(1)).

children.heading: Student loan payments not treated as plan contribution.—
children.label: ii Student loan payments not treated as plan contribution.—
children.text: Except as provided in clause (iii), a qualified student loan payment shall not be treated as a contribution to a plan under this title.

children.heading: Matching contribution rules
children.label: iii Matching contribution rules
children.text: Solely for purposes of meeting the requirements of paragraph (11)(B), (12), or (13) of this subsection, or paragraph (11)(B)(i)(II), (12)(B), (13)(D), or (16)(D) of subsection (k), a plan may treat a qualified student loan payment as an elective deferral or an elective contribution, whichever is applicable.

children.heading: Actual deferral percentage testing
children.label: iv Actual deferral percentage testing
children.text: In determining whether a plan meets the requirements of subsection (k)(3)(A)(ii) for a plan year, the plan may apply the requirements of such subsection separately with respect to all employees who receive matching contributions described in paragraph (4)(A)(iii) for the plan year.

children.heading: Employer may rely on employee certification
children.label: C Employer may rely on employee certification
children.text: The employer may rely on an employee certification of payment under paragraph (4)(D)(ii).

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: 14 Cross reference
children.text: For excise tax on certain excess contributions, see section 4979.

children.heading: Coordination with qualified domestic relations orders
children.label: n Coordination with qualified domestic relations orders
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such rules or regulations as may be necessary to coordinate the requirements of subsection (a)(13)(B) and section 414(p) (and the regulations issued by the Secretary of Labor thereunder) with the other provisions of this chapter.

children.heading: Special rules for applying nondiscrimination rules to protect older, longer service and grandfathered participants
children.label: o Special rules for applying nondiscrimination rules to protect older, longer service and grandfathered participants

children.heading: Testing of defined benefit plans with closed classes of participants
children.label: 1 Testing of defined benefit plans with closed classes of participants

children.heading: Benefits, rights, or features provided to closed classes
children.label: A Benefits, rights, or features provided to closed classes
i
children.text: for the plan year as of which the class closes and the 2 succeeding plan years, such benefits, rights, and features satisfy the requirements of subsection (a)(4) (without regard to this subparagraph but taking into account the rules of subparagraph (I)),

children.label: ii
children.text: after the date as of which the class was closed, any plan amendment which modifies the closed class or the benefits, rights, and features provided to such closed class does not discriminate significantly in favor of highly compensated employees, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the class was closed before , or the plan is described in subparagraph (C).April 5, 2017

children.heading: Aggregate testing with defined contribution plans permitted on a benefits basis
children.label: B Aggregate testing with defined contribution plans permitted on a benefits basis
I
children.text: provides matching contributions (as defined in subsection (m)(4)(A)),

children.label: II
children.text: provides annuity contracts described in section 403(b) which are purchased with matching contributions or nonelective contributions, or

children.label: III
children.text: consists of an employee stock ownership plan (within the meaning of section 4975(e)(7)) or a tax credit employee stock ownership plan (within the meaning of section 409(a)).

children.heading: Special rules for matching contributions
children.label: ii Special rules for matching contributions
I
children.text: such defined benefit plan must also be aggregated with any portion of such defined contribution plan which provides elective deferrals described in subparagraph (A) or (C) of section 402(g)(3), and

children.label: II
children.text: such matching contributions shall be treated in the same manner as nonelective contributions, including for purposes of applying the rules of subsection ().l

children.label: I
children.text: the plan provides benefits to a closed class of participants,

children.label: II
children.text: for the plan year as of which the class closes and the 2 succeeding plan years, the plan satisfies the requirements of section 410(b) and subsection (a)(4) (without regard to this subparagraph but taking into account the rules of subparagraph (I)),

children.label: III
children.text: after the date as of which the class was closed, any plan amendment which modifies the closed class or the benefits provided to such closed class does not discriminate significantly in favor of highly compensated employees, and

children.label: IV
children.text: the class was closed before , or the plan is described in subparagraph (C).April 5, 2017

children.label: i
children.text: such plan has been in effect for at least 5 years as of the date the class is closed, and

children.label: ii
children.text: during the 5-year period preceding the date the class is closed, there has not been a substantial increase in the coverage or value of the benefits, rights, or features described in subparagraph (A) or in the coverage or benefits under the plan described in subparagraph (B)(iii) (whichever is applicable).

children.heading: Determination of substantial increase for benefits, rights, and features
children.label: D Determination of substantial increase for benefits, rights, and features
i
children.text: the number of participants covered by such benefits, rights, or features on the date such period ends is more than 50 percent greater than the number of such participants on the first day of the plan year in which such period began, or

children.label: ii
children.text: such benefits, rights, and features have been modified by 1 or more plan amendments in such a way that, as of the date the class is closed, the value of such benefits, rights, and features to the closed class as a whole is substantially greater than the value as of the first day of such 5-year period, solely as a result of such amendments.

children.heading: Determination of substantial increase for aggregate testing on benefits basis
children.label: E Determination of substantial increase for aggregate testing on benefits basis
i
children.text: the number of participants benefitting under the plan on the date such period ends is more than 50 percent greater than the number of such participants on the first day of the plan year in which such period began, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the average benefit provided to such participants on the date such period ends is more than 50 percent greater than the average benefit provided on the first day of the plan year in which such period began.

children.label: i
children.text: who became participants as a result of a merger, acquisition, or similar event which occurred during the 7-year period preceding the date the class is closed, or

children.label: ii
children.text: who became participants by reason of a merger of the plan with another plan which had been in effect for at least 5 years as of the date of the merger,

children.heading: Rules relating to average benefit
children.label: G Rules relating to average benefit
i
children.text: the average benefit provided to participants under the plan will be treated as having remained the same between the 2 dates described in subparagraph (E)(ii) if the benefit formula applicable to such participants has not changed between such dates, and

children.label: I
children.text: the total amount determined under section 430(b)(1)(A)(i) for all participants benefitting under the plan for the plan year in which the 5-year period described in subparagraph (E) ends, exceeds

children.label: II
children.text: the total amount determined under section 430(b)(1)(A)(i) for all such participants for such plan year, by using the benefit formula in effect for each such participant for the first plan year in such 5-year period,

children.heading: Treatment as single plan
children.label: H Treatment as single plan
children.text: For purposes of subparagraphs (E) and (G), a plan described in section 413(c) shall be treated as a single plan rather than as separate plans maintained by each employer in the plan.

children.label: i
children.text: In applying section 410(b)(6)(C), the closing of the class of participants shall not be treated as a significant change in coverage under section 410(b)(6)(C)(i)(II).

children.label: ii
children.text: 2 or more plans shall not fail to be eligible to be aggregated and treated as a single plan solely by reason of having different plan years.

children.label: iii
children.text: Changes in the employee population shall be disregarded to the extent attributable to individuals who become employees or cease to be employees, after the date the class is closed, by reason of a merger, acquisition, divestiture, or similar event.

children.label: iv
children.text: Aggregation and all other testing methodologies otherwise applicable under subsection (a)(4) and section 410(b) may be taken into account.

children.label: i
children.text: subparagraph (A)(i) or (B)(iii)(II), whichever is applicable, if the original plan was still within the 3-year period described in such subparagraph at the time of the spin off, and

children.label: ii
children.text: subparagraph (A)(ii) or (B)(iii)(III), whichever is applicable,

children.heading: Testing on a benefits basis
children.label: A Testing on a benefits basis
i
children.text: such defined contribution plan provides make-whole contributions to a closed class of participants whose accruals under a defined benefit plan have been reduced or eliminated,

children.label: ii
children.text: for the plan year of the defined contribution plan as of which the class eligible to receive such make-whole contributions closes and the 2 succeeding plan years, such closed class of participants satisfies the requirements of section 410(b)(2)(A)(i) (determined by applying the rules of paragraph (1)(I)),

children.label: iii
children.text: after the date as of which the class was closed, any plan amendment to the defined contribution plan which modifies the closed class or the allocations, benefits, rights, and features provided to such closed class does not discriminate significantly in favor of highly compensated employees, and

children.label: iv
children.text: the class was closed before , or the defined benefit plan under clause (i) is described in paragraph (1)(C) (as applied for purposes of paragraph (1)(B)(iii)(IV)).April 5, 2017

children.heading: Aggregation with plans including matching contributions
children.label: B Aggregation with plans including matching contributions
I
children.text: provides matching contributions (as defined in subsection (m)(4)(A)),

children.label: II
children.text: provides annuity contracts described in section 403(b) which are purchased with matching contributions or nonelective contributions, or

children.label: III
children.text: consists of an employee stock ownership plan (within the meaning of section 4975(e)(7)) or a tax credit employee stock ownership plan (within the meaning of section 409(a)).

children.heading: Special rules for matching contributions
children.label: ii Special rules for matching contributions
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of paragraph (1)(B)(ii) shall apply for purposes of clause (i).

children.heading: Special rules for testing defined contribution plan features providing matching contributions to certain older, longer service participants
children.label: C Special rules for testing defined contribution plan features providing matching contributions to certain older, longer service participants
children.text: In the case of a defined contribution plan which provides benefits, rights, or features to a closed class of participants whose accruals under a defined benefit plan have been reduced or eliminated, the plan shall not fail to satisfy the requirements of subsection (a)(4) solely by reason of the composition of the closed class or the benefits, rights, or features provided to such closed class if the defined contribution plan and defined benefit plan otherwise meet the requirements of subparagraph (A) but for the fact that the make-whole contributions under the defined contribution plan are made in whole or in part through matching contributions.

children.heading: Spun-off plans
children.label: D Spun-off plans
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, if a portion of a defined contribution plan described in subparagraph (A) or (C) is spun off to another employer, the treatment under subparagraph (A) or (C) of the spun-off plan shall continue with respect to the other employer if such plan continues to comply with the requirements of clauses (ii) (if the original plan was still within the 3-year period described in such clause at the time of the spin off) and (iii) of subparagraph (A), as determined for purposes of subparagraph (A) or (C), whichever is applicable.

children.heading: Make-whole contributions
children.label: A Make-whole contributions
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in paragraph (2)(C), the term “make-whole contributions” means nonelective allocations for each employee in the class which are reasonably calculated, in a consistent manner, to replace some or all of the retirement benefits which the employee would have received under the defined benefit plan and any other plan or qualified cash or deferred arrangement under subsection (k)(2) if no change had been made to such defined benefit plan and such other plan or arrangement. For purposes of the preceding sentence, consistency shall not be required with respect to employees who were subject to different benefit formulas under the defined benefit plan.

children.heading: References to closed class of participants
children.label: B References to closed class of participants
children.text: References to a closed class of participants and similar references to a closed class shall include arrangements under which 1 or more classes of participants are closed, except that 1 or more classes of participants closed on different dates shall not be aggregated for purposes of determining the date any such class was closed.

children.heading: Highly compensated employee
children.label: C Highly compensated employee
children.text: The term “highly compensated employee” has the meaning given such term in section 414(q).

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: p Cross reference
children.text: For exemption from tax of a trust qualified under this section, see section 501(a).
